world.title: 2 youths in Dominican Republic honored for academic achievements
world.description: Two Latter-day Saint young women from the Dominican Republic were recently recognized for their academic achievements.

world.title: Oil tanker ablaze after projectile strike off Dubai coast as new warning issued
world.description: The movement of tankers through the Strait of Hormuz has been a contentious issue since the conflict in the Middle East started more than one month ago, disrupting oil markets

world.title: Gulf States pressure Donald Trump to continue war with Iran
world.description: The Gulf States, according to Israeli and Gulf officials, believe that Iran hasn't been weakened enough yet for the regime to stop being a regional threat.

world.description: After early complaints over lack of warning and ignored concerns, Gulf allies now see the war as a rare chance to weaken Iran’s leadership and are urging Washington to press ahead with the campaign

world.title: Dezi Freeman holding gun belonging to slain officer when he was killed
world.description: It's been revealed a gun pulled by Freeman in his final moments belonged to one of the officers the self-proclaimed sovereign citizen executed last year.

world.title: Cos. Say UK Court Ruling Aids $440M Zimbabwe Award Bid
world.description: Two forestry and sawmill companies along with a Swiss-German family have told a D.C. federal court that a recent decision from Britain's top court rejecting Zimbabwe's sovereign immunity defense in a related case shows that $440 million of arbitral awards can be enforced.

world.description: As ties with Washington sour, China is reviving a cold war strategy to defend against a US attack

world.title: Inside the cockpit of RAF tanker during defensive mission against Iranian drones
world.description: Defence correspondent Jonathan Beale flies onboard an RAF Voyager as it refuels jets in the Middle East.

business.title: Mortgage holders warned as Westpac forecasts cash rate to hit 18
business.description: Australia faces its highest cash rate since the Global Financial Crisis as one of the big banks predicts aggressive RBA action that could devastate mortgage holders.

business.title: War on Iran a 'bazooka' through government's LNG plan - gentailer CEO
business.description: The Energy Minister is expressing confidence in the government's plans to build a liquefied natural gas terminal, even as concerns mount.

Chinese coffee brands need more than low prices to compete with global players: analysts
business.description: Luckin, Lucky Cup, Cotti and Nowwa are expanding quickly overseas, but analysts warn success depends on consumer experience and quality.

business.title: JD.com And Ceconomy Mega Deal Delayed By Austrian Regulators

business.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adult employees
business.description: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

business.title: Coal Is Back - and Japan Is Driving the Rally
business.description: Tokyo is signalling a broader role for coal in the power mix as it seeks a near-term buffer against surging LNG prices and supply uncertainty.

business.title: Moves like Jag? Errr.. Yes, actually. New GT vs XJ and E-Type icons

science.title: Huge solar flare no threat to Artemis 2 astronaut launch to the moon, NASA says
science.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronaut mission to the moon is "go" for launch on April 1 and won't be affected by a huge solar flare.

science.title: Animals are powerful landscape engineers shaping the Earth's surface, global study finds
science.description: Wild animals are not just inhabitants of the natural world. Many also act as natural landscape engineers, reshaping Earth's surface as they burrow, feed, and build shelters that move soil and sediment across ecosystems. From animals disturbing riverbeds to burrowing species redistributing soil, wildlife constantly modifies the physical structure of landscapes through everyday activities.

science.title: Quantum Computing Built An Impossible Molecule - With Big Implications
science.description: An international team of scientists proved the never-before-seen molecule's exotic nature using a quantum computer, potentially ushering in new scientific opportunities.

science.title: Oldest Carbon-rich Stars Open a Window to Early Cosmic Chemistry
science.description: Astronomers studying the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Pictor II have found an extremely chemically peculiar star that contains traces of elements created by the first stars in the Universe. It's called PicII-503, a

science.title: Live updates: Artemis II countdown begins as 80% ‘Go’ weather forecast holds for Florida
science.description: NASA is targeting an April 1 launch for the historic Artemis II mission, with meteorologists currently tracking an 80% "Go" weather forecast at Kennedy Space Center. Follow our live updates for real-time radar, cloud thickness analysis, and the latest countdown status as four astronauts prepare to journey around the Moon.

science.title: Light switch for life: Controlling molecular droplets with UV
science.description: Biomolecular condensates are tiny, droplet-like structures made up of molecules that help organize key processes in living organisms. Because they are so small and constantly changing, it has been difficult for scientists to measure their physical properties or control how they behave. Leiden researchers at the Mashaghi Lab have now discovered a surprising new way to shape and control tiny droplets of molecules found in living organisms. The breakthrough could lead to smarter biomaterials, improve drug delivery and even new insights into the emergence of life on Earth. The work is published in Nature Communications.

science.title: Money worries and job dissatisfaction drove Europe's populist boom, research suggests
science.description: While immigration is often blamed for the rise of populism, it was cost of living and male job dissatisfaction that played a major role in the European surge in support for populist politics a decade ago, according to a University of Cambridge social scientist.

science.title: Tasmanian tigers discovered in Indigenous rock art in Australia, suggesting these marsupials lived there much longer than thought
science.description: Around 14 new rock-art depictions of thylacines, also known as Tasmanian tigers, have been found in northern Australia.

ai.title: Big Tech Faces Financial Pressure Amid AI Investment Boom
ai.description: As high market-based interest rates surge due to Middle East conflicts and resultant energy shocks, U.S. tech firms are facing rising borrowing costs amidst their significant AI investments. Predicted to spend over 2% of GDP this year on AI infrastructure, tech companies are increasingly relying on credit markets to fund this unprecedented capex surge.

ai.title: Artificial Intelligence: Revolutionizing Every Aspect of Life - Big Tech Faces Financial Pressure Amid AI Investment Boom
ai.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is rapidly redefining the way we live, work, and interact. No longer confined to the realm of science fiction, AI is now a driving force behind innovations across all sectors— from healthcare and finance to entertainment and manufacturing. Its impact is far-reaching, transforming industries, reshaping economies, and influencing the way we navigate daily life.
